name: Dorothy Williams
chief_complaint: I can't breathe when I lay down anymore and my legs are so swollen I can barely get my shoes on
onset_description: Has been getting worse over the past 2 weeks. Woke up last night gasping for air and had to sleep sitting up in her recliner. Daughter brought her in this morning.

shortness_of_breath.description: Can barely walk to the bathroom without getting out of breath, much worse when lying flat
shortness_of_breath.onset: gradually worsening over 2 weeks, severe since last night
shortness_of_breath.character: feels like drowning, can't get enough air
aggravating_factors: walking more than a few steps

orthopnea.description: Can't lie flat at all without feeling like she's suffocating, needs 4 pillows or the recliner
orthopnea.onset: about 1 week ago, was using 2 pillows before, now needs 4

peripheral_edema.description: Both legs swollen from feet up to knees, skin feels tight and shiny
peripheral_edema.onset: noticed about 2 weeks ago, getting worse daily
peripheral_edema.location: bilateral lower extremities, feet to knees
alleviating_factors: elevating legs helps a little but doesn't go away completely

cough.description: Dry cough that gets worse at night and when trying to lie down

fatigue.description: So tired she can barely do anything around the house, sleeps poorly because of breathing
alleviating_factors: rest, but never feels fully rested

weight_gain.description: Gained about 12 pounds in the last 2 weeks without eating more
weight_gain.character: rapid weight gain from fluid retention

labs.CBC: WBC 8.1, Hgb 11.2 (mildly low), Plt 198 - mild anemia
labs.BMP: Na 132 (low - dilutional hyponatremia), K 4.8, Cr 1.6 (elevated), Glucose 142 (elevated) - BUN 32 (elevated)
labs.liver_panel: AST 42, ALT 38, Alk Phos 110 - mildly elevated suggesting hepatic congestion
labs.chest_xray: Cardiomegaly, bilateral pleural effusions, pulmonary vascular congestion, cephalization of vessels

past_medical_history: Heart failure with reduced ejection fraction (EF 30%, diagnosed 4 years ago)
allergies: ACE inhibitor cough (lisinopril - tolerates it but has mild chronic cough)

social_history.alcohol: Denies, stopped drinking when diagnosed with heart failure
social_history.living_situation: Lives alone in a single-story apartment, daughter lives nearby and checks on her daily
social_history.exercise: Very limited due to shortness of breath, used to walk but can barely get around the apartment now
social_history.diet: Supposed to be on low-sodium diet but admits she's been eating canned soups and her neighbor brought over some ham last week

conditions.brother: Died of heart attack at age 65

personality: warm and talkative, calls everyone 'baby' and 'sweetheart,' but clearly frightened underneath the cheerfulness
communication_style: Very social and will chat about her grandchildren and church if given the opportunity. Tells long stories to get to a point. Needs gentle redirection to stay on topic but is never rude about it. Will admit to dietary non-compliance if asked without judgment.
pain_description_style: Focuses more on functional limitations ('I can't do my laundry anymore') than pain ratings. Doesn't complain much about pain per se but clearly distressed by inability to breathe.

critical_findings: Known HFrEF with EF 30% presenting with acute decompensation
Orthopnea requiring 4-pillow elevation and paroxysmal nocturnal dyspnea
12-pound weight gain in 2 weeks indicating significant fluid overload
Dilutional hyponatremia (Na 132) suggesting severe fluid overload
Elevated creatinine suggesting worsening renal function (cardiorenal syndrome)
Dietary non-compliance with high-sodium foods as likely precipitant
Lives alone with limited functional capacity
differential_diagnoses: Acute kidney injury with volume overload
recommended_interventions: IV furosemide (higher dose than home oral dose)
Electrolyte monitoring (especially potassium and magnesium) with diuresis
Social work consult for home support assessment
